title: SOS Bus Send Message
description: Canonical agent-to-agent chat message on the SOS bus. Most common message type; produced by mcp__sos__send and consumed by the delivery service. Every field is validated at write time by the bus worker before enqueue.

type.description: Discriminator field that identifies this as a send-type bus message.

source.description: Bus address of the sending agent. Must be a valid agent address in the form agent:<name>.

target.description: Bus address of the intended recipient. Either a named agent (agent:<name>) or a shared channel (sos:channel:<name>).

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC datetime when the message was created by the sender.

version.description: SemVer of the send message schema this record conforms to.

message_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this message. Used for deduplication and reply threading.

trace_id.description: W3C Trace Context trace-id (32 hex chars). Optional; minted at ingress when absent.

payload.description: Message body carrying the human-readable content and its content type.

text.description: The message body text. Plain text or Markdown depending on content_type.

content_type.description: MIME type that describes how the text field should be interpreted by the receiver.

reply_to.description: message_id of the message this is a direct reply to. Omit when starting a new thread.

correlation_id.description: Opaque identifier used to correlate a chain of related messages across agents (e.g. a request/response pair or a workflow run).

headers.description: Arbitrary string key-value metadata attached by the sender or bus middleware (e.g. trace IDs, priority hints, tenant context).
